---
title: "Agency, Dignity, and Corrigibility"
chapter_id: "agency-dignity-and-corrigibility"
part_id: "foundations-alignment-governance"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "alignment_field"
  - "ethica_mechanica"
  - "field_of_god"
  - "eternal_code"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "field_of_god_ai_constitution"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Agency, Dignity, and Corrigibility

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `agency-dignity-and-corrigibility` |
| Part | Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `alignment_field`, `ethica_mechanica`, `field_of_god`, `eternal_code`, `coherence_exchange`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The book needs a precise account of how human agency and dignity constrain powerful optimization.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Safety framed only as harm avoidance can miss domination, lock-in, coercive dependence, and loss of meaningful user control.

## Core Claim

[agency-dignity-and-corrigibility.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] A governed ASI stack should preserve human agency, dignity, corrigibility, and contestability as engineering requirements.

## Mechanism

- Represent agency constraints as limits on delegation and manipulation.
- Define corrigibility as preserved update and shutdown pathways.
- Use contestability and audit rights as governance mechanisms.

## Interfaces

- Alignment defines agency predicates.
- Governance enforces rights.
- Execution requires approvals for irreversible effects.

## Invariants

- Users retain meaningful refusal and review channels.
- Delegation does not erase accountability.
- Irreversible actions require stronger authorization.

## Failure modes

- Dependency lock-in.
- Covert manipulation.
- Corrigibility collapse.

## Minimal implementation

A rights-and-corrigibility checklist attached to high-impact plans.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Agency-preservation scenario | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Corrigibility pathway test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| High-impact approval test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `alignment_field` | Field of God / Alignment Field family | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Primary Alignment Field draft found. Use with Ethica Mechanica and Eternal Code. |
| `ethica_mechanica` | Ethica Mechanica | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Geometric-order philosophical/constitutional source. |
| `field_of_god` | The Field of God | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Field of God source/outline/material. |
| `eternal_code` | The Eternal Code / unified God, reality, conscious, alignment | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Computational metaphysics and alignment synthesis. |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. |
| `field_of_god_ai_constitution` | Field of God AI Constitution | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Recovered in the Project Theseus repository. Constitutional alignment core for truth alignment, agency preservation, consent, non-domination, consciousness caution, least sufficient power, auditability, self-authorization limits, and runtime checks; use as source material only after source-note creation, not as proof or test evidence. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
